back_translation: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
'Speak to Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, and let him lift up the censers from the midst of the burning, and the fire, scatter afar; for they have been sanctified.'

The censers of these sinners at the cost of their lives — they shall make them hammered plates, an overlay for the altar; for they offered them before the LORD, and they were sanctified; and they shall be a sign to the sons of Israel.
And Eleazar the priest took the bronze censers which those who were burned had offered, and they hammered them out as an overlay for the altar.

A memorial to the sons of Israel, that a stranger who is not from the seed of Aaron should not approach to burn incense before the LORD, and not become like Korah and like his congregation — as the LORD had spoken to him by the hand of Moses.
And on the next day, the whole congregation of the sons of Israel grumbled against Moses and against Aaron, saying: 'You have killed the people of the LORD.'
And it happened, when the congregation gathered against Moses and against Aaron, that they turned toward the Tent of Meeting, and behold, the cloud covered it, and the glory of the LORD appeared.
And Moses and Aaron came to the front of the Tent of Meeting.
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
'Lift yourselves up from the midst of this congregation, and I will consume them in an instant.' And they fell on their faces.

And Moses said to Aaron: 'Take the censer and put on it fire from the altar, and place incense, and go quickly to the congregation and make atonement for them, for wrath has gone out from before the LORD; the plague has begun.'
And Aaron took it as Moses had spoken, and he ran into the midst of the assembly, and behold, the plague had begun among the people; and he put on the incense and made atonement for the people.
And he stood between the dead and the living, and the plague was stopped.
And those who died in the plague were 14,700 — besides those who died on account of the matter of Korah.
And Aaron returned to Moses to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and the plague was stopped.
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

'Speak to the sons of Israel and take from them a staff, a staff for each ancestral house, from all their leaders by their ancestral houses — twelve staffs; each man, his name you shall write on his staff.
And Aaron's name you shall write on the staff of Levi, for one staff is for the head of their ancestral house.
And you shall place them in the Tent of Meeting before the Testimony, where I meet with you.'
'And it shall be — the man whom I choose, his staff shall sprout; and I will cause to subside from upon Me the grumblings of the sons of Israel which they grumble against you.'

And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel, and all their leaders gave him a staff — a staff for each leader, a staff for each leader, by their ancestral houses — twelve staffs; and Aaron's staff was among their staffs.
And Moses placed the staffs before the LORD in the Tent of the Testimony.

And it happened, on the next day, that Moses came to the Tent of the Testimony, and behold, Aaron's staff for the house of Levi had sprouted, and brought forth a bud, and blossomed a blossom, and ripened almonds.
And Moses brought out all the staffs from before the LORD to all the sons of Israel; and they saw, and each man took his own staff.

And the LORD said to Moses: 'Return Aaron's staff before the Testimony, for safekeeping, as a sign to the rebellious sons, so that you may put an end to their grumblings against Me, and they will not die.'
And Moses did so; as the LORD commanded him, so he did.
And the sons of Israel said to Moses, saying: 'Behold, we are dying, we are perishing, all of us are perishing!'
'Everyone who comes near, who comes near to the tabernacle of the LORD, will die. Are we to be finished off in perishing?'
